page: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with multi-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Additionally, the server includes 945 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates that the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier performance in single-core operations, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, all of which rank in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, however, falls into the average category, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark memory scores, also aligns with the middle 50% of tested systems. For LLM inference workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M models, but performance drops to average for 7B models and is weak for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ads_v7 offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated high-frequency compute cores with a substantial local storage allocation. This configuration is suitable for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded performance and local scratch space. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose web serving, database operations, small-scale language model inference, and application servers requiring moderate memory and dedicated CPU resources.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks. However, its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. It also demonstrates strong to top-tier performance for small-scale LLM inference, though speed degrades significantly on larger models. Lacking a GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a balance of dedicated compute, local storage, and high single-thread speed. It is well-suited for general-purpose web serving, database operations, and application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, offering strong single-core speed.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high single-core performance, and integrated local SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 945 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database performance profiles
Suitable for general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and application servers
